prompt_md: You lead a team building a **bulk receipt upload** feature in an **AI-driven SDLC**: coding assistants generate most of the code, draft tests, and propose refactors. You want to manage the delivery soundly instead of assuming AI simply makes it 3× faster. **Select every option that is a sound way to run delivery in an AI-driven SDLC.**

choices: Estimate the review, integration, and verification work explicitly, budgeting for the last-10% edge cases — not just the fast generation
Treat code as done once it compiles and the happy-path demo works, skipping review to preserve velocity
Keep a human accountable for reviewing, verifying, and owning correctness and security of whatever the AI generates
Quote a delivery date 3× sooner up front because the assistant writes most of the code
Track real throughput (cycle time, defect-escape rate, value shipped) rather than output like lines generated or PR count
Make human review of generated code, automated tests, and security/type checks the definition of done before anything merges
